other_observers: Lisa Edwards on 07/01/2012 and Mark Peterson 07/15/2012.; Lisa Edwards and I first found the bird on 07/01/2012 and it was seen by Cole Wild, Mark Peterson, and Stan Oswald as well.
description: Lisa Edwards and I were birding at Lake Holbrook, and I started looking at the cormorants perched in the trees, and quickly noticed a smaller cormorant will a longer tail sitting in the trees in the middle of the lake, with bigger Double-crested Cormorants. I asked Lisa to look at the cormorant in my scope, and she also saw the smaller cormorant, and agreed that it was a Neotropic Cormorant. Besides for the size of the bird, being smaller than the other cormorants, also the bird had longer tail, thinner neck, and smaller bill. I've included a photo of the bird, taken by Cole Wild. I saw the bird again with Mark Peterson on July 15th.Behaviors: Sitting in dead trees with other cormorants.Call: nonePlumage: immature
